title: FAQ Additions — add 3 new FAQ entries and use FAQDataList as offline fallback
commit_message: feat: add password-reset, streaming-quality, and payment-methods FAQs — use FAQDataList as fallback when API returns empty or errors
changed_symbols: FAQDataList (modified — 3 new entries added: ids 6, 7, 8 covering password reset, streaming quality, payment methods)
FrequentlyAskedQuestions useEffect (modified — FAQAPI response now falls back to FAQDataList when items array is empty or API throws)

what_the_diff_does.before: Help Center shows FAQ items fetched from FAQAPI. If the API returns an empty list or throws, the FAQ section is hidden entirely (faqItems stays []). FAQDataList had 5 entries but was unused by the component.
what_the_diff_does.after: Help Center always shows at least 8 FAQ items. If the API returns a non-empty list, those items are shown. If the API returns empty or fails, the component falls back to the local FAQDataList (which now has 8 entries including 3 new ones). Users always see content.
what_the_diff_does.visible_change: Three new FAQ entries appear in the Help Center: 'How do I reset my account password?', 'What streaming quality does iStream support?', and 'What payment methods does iStream accept?'. The FAQ section no longer disappears when the API is unavailable.
what_the_diff_does.compile_note: FAQDataList is imported from a sibling file in the same directory — no new npm packages or external dependencies introduced.

CRITICAL.reason: faq-data.pure.test.js declares both changed files as @source and tests every changed symbol: FAQDataList size (2 tests), new entries ids 6/7/8 (8 tests), fallback logic (6 tests). All 16 are CRITICAL.
tests: FAQDataList — size > contains 8 entries after the 3 new FAQs are added
FAQDataList — size > every entry has a unique id
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry with id "6" exists
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry with id "7" exists
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry with id "8" exists
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry 6 sectionHeader mentions password reset
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry 7 sectionHeader mentions streaming quality
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > entry 8 sectionHeader mentions payment methods
FAQDataList — new entries (ids 6, 7, 8) > all 3 new entries have non-empty sectionContent
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > returns API items when API responds with a non-empty array
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > falls back to FAQDataList when API responds with an empty array
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > falls back to FAQDataList when API response has no items field
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > falls back to FAQDataList when API response is null
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > falls back to FAQDataList when API throws an error
FAQ fallback logic — resolveFaqItems > fallback list has 8 items so users always see content

HIGH.reason: auth-reducers.feature.test.js covers the navigation layer which includes faqScreen. user-profile.feature.test.js covers the profile/menu area where Help Center is launched from. Both must pass to confirm navigation to FAQ still works.

SKIP.reason: All remaining test files do not declare FAQDataList.js or FrequentlyAskedQuestions.js as a source.

coverage_gaps.gap: No existing tests validated the contents of FAQDataList or its new entries.
No existing tests covered the API-empty / API-error fallback to FAQDataList.

jest_run_note: faq-data.pure.test.js runs 15 CRITICAL tests for FAQDataList additions and fallback logic. auth-reducers and user-profile run 35 HIGH tests confirming Help Center navigation is unaffected.
